LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES AS COMMISSIONER OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER BREACHED A SPECIFIC MANDATORY DUTY. (SCHWARTZ V. POIZNER (2010) 187 CAL. APP. 4TH 572). A WRIT OF MANDATE CANNOT BE BASED ON GENERAL ENFORCEMENT PROVISIONS OR STATUTES INVOLVING THE DEFENDANT'S EXERCISE OF DISCRETION. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE DEFENDANT ABUSED HIS DISCRETION BY ACTING ARBITRARILY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-24-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Mtn To Dismiss
Off Calendar Jun-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Anne Michelle Burr, Deputy Attorney General, 415.703.1403, for defendant Dave Jones as the Commissioner of Insurance; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES AS THE COMMISSIONER OF INSURANCE'S Motion To Dismiss is granted, no opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anne Michelle Burr, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.1403, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES AS COMMISSIONER OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
